page: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and standard CPU execution.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6a.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests and Passmark CPU scores. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only cached bandwidth and strong large-block read/write speeds. Computational workloads such as software compilation with Clang and ray tracing also register in the top 10% of benchmarked systems. For database and memory-bound tasks, the instance delivers top-tier Redis SET throughput and strong static web serving capabilities.

The instance offers high compute density and strong multi-threaded performance, making it highly cost-efficient for parallel processing workloads that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration. However, the lack of local storage requires network-based storage solutions, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. The DDR4 memory architecture, while highly capable, represents an older generation compared to DDR5 systems. This server is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, including software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for Passmark CPU and stress-ng multi-core benchmarks. It also exhibits strong memory bandwidth and high Redis SET throughput. The c6a.32xlarge is highly cost-efficient for compute-dense, parallel workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving, though users must account for the latency of network-attached storage due to the absence of local disks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized platform delivering 128 vCPUs of dedicated AMD EPYC performance for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU.
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.
Top-tier multi-core performance in Passmark and stress-ng benchmarks.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity.
Optimized for software compilation, ray tracing, and high-volume web serving.
No local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage.
